projects.title: Text-to-Image Generation with Amazon Bedrock
projects.text: This project is a React-based web application that uses AWS services to generate and display AI-created images from textual prompts. Users interact with the underlying Amazon Bedrock model through an AWS Lambda function, which is exposed via Amazon API Gateway. The application processes requests and dynamically displays the generated images in a responsive user interface. The project focuses as much on the system design (React frontend, serverless backend, API Gateway and IAM) as on the generative model itself, reflecting an event-driven architecture.
projects.footerLanguage: Amazon Bedrock · AWS Lambda · API Gateway · React · Terraform

projects.title: ISS Tracker
projects.text: A real‑time 2D tracker for the International Space Station that consumes live orbital data from a public API and displays the ISS on an interactive world map, showing its current position, ground track, footprint, key statistics, and whether the station is currently visible, eclipsed or observable in daylight.
projects.footerLanguage: JavaScript · Web Application · Real-time Data · APIs

projects.title: CNN Landscape Classifier
projects.text: A convolutional neural network (CNN) built with TensorFlow and Keras to classify landscape images into three categories: desert, forest and polar. The project covers data preprocessing, model training and evaluation to benchmark performance across classes.

projects.title: Naive Bayes Text Classifier
projects.text: A Naive Bayes text classifier that labels input articles as either BUSINESS or SPORT. The project demonstrates a simple end-to-end NLP pipeline in JavaScript, including tokenisation, feature extraction, model training and prediction.

projects.title: Cartoon-Photo Classifier (Model)
projects.text: Two comparative convolutional neural networks (CNNs) built with TensorFlow and Keras to predict whether an image is a cartoon or a photo. One model is trained from scratch on the provided dataset, while the other uses transfer learning from a pre-trained network. The transfer learning approach significantly improves accuracy on a relatively small dataset and converges within only a handful of epochs.

projects.title: Cartoon-Photo Classifier (Deployment)
projects.text:
Two techniques are explored for deploying the pre-trained cartoon–photo model:
  • Python Flask applications exposing the TensorFlow–Keras model either as an API or as a web app. Photos are uploaded and the model predicts whether the image is a cartoon or a photo, along with the associated confidence score.
  • TensorFlow Serving, run inside a Docker container, acting as a wrapper for the model to provide an HTTP API for querying predictions.

The emphasis is on clean deployment patterns – exposing models via well-defined HTTP APIs and containerised serving. The deployment code can be found here.

projects.title: Shape Mapping
projects.text: A demonstration of how a self-organising map (SOM), a type of artificial neural network also known as a Kohonen map, maps a randomised 2D mesh over a 3D point cloud. The project explores how SOMs can learn and represent the underlying structure of complex shapes.

projects.title: Self-Organising Structure
projects.text: Randomised autonomous agents self-organise to build a structured pattern based on their ability to seek out a target and adjust their velocity and acceleration accordingly. The project illustrates emergent behaviour in multi-agent systems and generative structures.

projects.title: Autoencoder for Dimensionality Reduction
projects.text: A demo project using an autoencoder, built with TensorFlow and Keras, to project high-dimensional data into a 2D or 3D space for visualisation. Using the multivariate Mushroom Dataset (edible vs poisonous), it explores how class structure appears in latent space and shows that the reduced representation is not linearly separable.

projects.title: Support Vector Machine
projects.text: A support vector machine (SVM) applied to the Kaggle Diabetes Prediction Dataset to build a small risk-prediction model. The project compares feature sets and class-imbalance strategies, focusing on an end-to-end workflow from data cleaning and training to evaluation and result interpretation.

projects.title: TSP Distance Calculator
projects.text: A demonstration of a genetic algorithm applied to the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) to find an optimised route through a set of cities. The application reads city data from a file, computes routes and their lengths using the Haversine distance, and exposes parameters such as crossover rate, mutation rate, population size, maximum iterations and elitism.

projects.title: Image Segmentation with K-Nearest Neighbour
projects.text: An image segmentation demo that uses the k-nearest neighbour algorithm to group pixels into clusters, highlighting distinct regions in the image.
